title: The Invisible Threat
scene1Question: What is the most serious scientific concern in this situation?
scene1Correct: C: Nitrate concentration above safe limit
scene2Question: What should you recommend FIRST as the environmental chemist?
scene2Outcome: A: Increase chemical treatment at the water plant => SUCCESS (positive)
B: Shut down the factory temporarily => FAIL (low)
C: Conduct further testing and monitor nitrate trends => FAIL (medium)
D: Advise residents to boil water => SUCCESS (positive)

title: The Reaction Gone Wrong
scene1Question: What is the most likely cause of this runaway reaction?
scene1Correct: B: Cooling system failure preventing heat removal
scene2Question: What is the safest immediate action?
scene2Outcome: A: Add more reactants to dilute the mixture => FAIL (medium)
B: Emergency vent to release pressure => FAIL (medium)
C: Inject cold water directly into reactor => FAIL (medium)
D: Activate emergency quench system with inhibitor => SUCCESS (positive)

correct: C: Controlling temperature and using inhibitors

title: Acid Rain Alert

B: Add limestone to affected lakes to neutralize acid => SUCCESS (positive)
C: Relocate affected communities => SUCCESS (positive)
D: Implement emissions trading with gradual reduction targets => SUCCESS (positive)

title: The Unstable Slope
scene1Question: Which combination of factors creates the highest landslide risk?
scene1Correct: B: Clay soil, steep slope, removed vegetation, and heavy rain
scene2Question: What do you recommend as the immediate priority?
scene2Outcome: A: Monitor the cracks and wait for more data => FAIL (medium)
B: Evacuate all residents immediately => FAIL (low)
C: Install drainage pipes to reduce water buildup => SUCCESS (positive)
D: Build a retaining wall at the base => SUCCESS (positive)

exitMcq.question: Clay-rich soils increase landslide risk because they:
correct: B: Become slippery when saturated with water

exitMcq.question: Vegetation on slopes reduces landslide risk by:
correct: C: Root systems binding soil together

title: Invasive Species Crisis
scene1Question: Why is the water hyacinth particularly damaging to this ecosystem?
scene1Correct: B: It blocks sunlight and oxygen, disrupting the food web
scene2Question: What control strategy do you recommend?
scene2Outcome: A: Introduce a biological control agent (plant-eating insect) => SUCCESS (positive)
B: Chemical herbicide treatment of affected areas => FAIL (medium)
C: Manual removal with floating barriers to contain spread => SUCCESS (positive)
D: Drain the lake partially to expose and kill the plants => FAIL (low)

exitMcq.question: Invasive species typically outcompete native species because they:
correct: B: Lack natural predators and diseases in the new environment

exitMcq.question: A food web describes:
correct: B: The interconnected feeding relationships in an ecosystem

scene1Question: What is the primary cause of this grid crisis?
scene1Correct: B: Demand exceeds generation capacity by 3,000 MW

B: Request emergency power from neighboring grids => SUCCESS (positive)

title: Heat Loss in Buildings
scene1Question: Which area should be the primary focus for reducing heat loss?
scene1Correct: B: Windows (35% loss through conduction and radiation)
scene2Question: What upgrade package do you recommend?
scene2Outcome: A: Replace all windows with triple-pane glass => SUCCESS (positive)
B: Add interior storm windows (preserves historic exterior) => SUCCESS (positive)
C: Blow insulation into roof and seal air leaks => SUCCESS (positive)
D: Install a more powerful heating system => FAIL (low)

title: The Aspirin Crisis
scene1Question: The team needs 500 g of aspirin total (1000 tablets × 0.5 g each). Using the 1:1 mole ratio, how many grams of salicylic acid are needed?
scene1Correct: B: 383 g — using molar mass ratio (138/180 × 500)
scene2Question: What is the most scientifically responsible decision?
scene2Outcome: A: Produce tablets with less active ingredient to meet the order quantity => FAIL (medium)
B: Produce as many correct-dose tablets as possible and report the shortage => SUCCESS (positive)
C: Rush emergency order for more salicylic acid and delay production => SUCCESS (positive)
D: Add a filler material to make up the tablet weight to 500 mg => FAIL (medium)

title: Fuel Production Optimization
scene1Question: Convert the given masses into moles. How many moles of each reactant do you have?
scene1Correct: B: 64 g S = 2 mol,  64 g O₂ = 2 mol
scene2Question: Based on the balanced equation and your mole calculations, which substance is the limiting reactant?
scene2Outcome: A: Sulfur — it will run out first => FAIL (low)
B: Oxygen — it will run out first => SUCCESS (positive)
C: Neither — both are used completely (1:1 ratio, 2 mol each) => SUCCESS (positive)
D: Sulfur dioxide — it is produced in limited amounts => FAIL (low)

exitMcq.question: In a chemical reaction, what does the limiting reactant determine?
correct: B: The amount of product formed

exitMcq.question: A reaction uses 96 g of sulfur (M = 32 g/mol) and 64 g of oxygen (M = 32 g/mol). Which is the limiting reactant?
correct: B: Oxygen — because it provides fewer moles (2 mol vs 3 mol)

title: The Efficiency Report
scene1Question: What does the theoretical yield of 180 g represent in this process?
scene1Correct: B: The maximum amount of product expected from the reaction
scene2Question: What is the BEST way to determine how efficient the reaction was?
scene2Outcome: A: Compare actual yield to theoretical yield using percent yield formula => SUCCESS (positive)
B: Subtract actual yield from theoretical yield only (180 − 135 = 45 g lost) => SUCCESS (positive)
C: Divide theoretical yield by actual yield (180 ÷ 135) => SUCCESS (positive)
D: Ignore the difference since product was still formed => FAIL (medium)

exitMcq.question: What does a percent yield of 100% indicate?
correct: B: The maximum possible product was obtained

title: Mission Oxygen Failure
scene1Question: What is the fundamental chemistry behind the normal oxygen generation system?
scene1Correct: B: Electrolysis: using electricity to split H₂O into H₂ and O₂
scene2Question: Which emergency oxygen generation method should you implement?
scene2Outcome: A: Activate chemical oxygen generators (lithium perchlorate candles) => SUCCESS (positive)
B: Attempt repair of electrolysis unit using spare parts => SUCCESS (positive)
C: Reduce crew activity to minimal levels to conserve oxygen => FAIL (low)
D: Use hydrogen peroxide decomposition (2H₂O₂ → 2H₂O + O₂) => SUCCESS (positive)

exitMcq.question: The balanced equation for water electrolysis is:
Why is water an ideal source for oxygen generation in space?
correct: B: It can be recycled from crew waste and provides both drinking water and oxygen
